citations.description: Python docstring correctly states 'force bypasses GATES generally — including any gate added in the future'
Claiming and transitioning section — no mention of --force gate bypass semantics
Agent claim/transition guidance — no mention of --force semantics

findings.criterion: Public Python, CLI, MCP, user, and agent guidance consistently states that force bypasses all gates.
findings.detail: The Python library docstring (`src/rebar/_lib_writes.py` line 350: 'force bypasses GATES generally — including any gate added in the future') and the CLI internal usage strings (`transition.py` lines 43–46, `claim.py` lines 30–31) all correctly state the forward-looking gate-bypass semantics. However, the user guide (`docs/user-guide.md`) contains no documentation of `--force` or force semantics at all (it only references gate protocols via a pointer to plan-review-gate.md), and the agent guidance (`AGENTS.md`) likewise has no mention of `--force` semantics. The MCP intentionally does not expose force (documented in `_mcp_writes.py` lines 119–121), so MCP is correctly described as not having force — but the criterion explicitly requires 'user, and agent guidance' to state the bypass semantics. Neither surface does so.
findings.remediation: Add a brief section to `docs/user-guide.md` (under 'Claiming and transitioning' or 'The quality gates as you experience them') and a one-liner to `AGENTS.md` stating that `--force` (or `force=True` in the library) bypasses any enabled start-work gate — including gates added in the future — and that the open-children close prohibition is a ticket-system invariant that cannot be bypassed by force.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
